data.narrative:
Firefly Aerospace, a public Space & Aerospace company founded by Tom Markusic, carries a current valuation of $6.3B following an $868M IPO. Valuation analysis relies on a three-peer comparable set within the same sector and stage: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Astroscale ($900M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across these three public Space & Aerospace operators is $7.0B, with a range spanning $900M to $37.0B. A public-comp anchoring method using P/S multiples (sector median 59.8x across six tickers including RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, and SATL) cannot be applied, as the target's revenue is not disclosed. This is typical for private-stage companies; Firefly's public status does not resolve the data gap in available materials.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $6.0B to $8.0B, centered on the $7.0B peer median. Firefly's current $6.3B valuation sits at the lower end of this band, approximately 10% below the peer median. Key uncertainties stem from the wide dispersion within the peer set—a 41x range from floor to ceiling—reflecting heterogeneity in stage, revenue, and operational maturity within the Space & Aerospace sector. The scarcity of applicable methods (one of two) limits confidence; revenue disclosure would enable P/S triangulation and narrow the range materially.
